candidate_summary: 4-fold rotational equivariance defect-detection for per-head attention feature map: d_h = max_θ ||F_h − R_θ·F_h||_F, with group-averaging repair F_h^repair = (1/4)·Σ_θ R_θ·F_h. Inspired by Persian carpet medallion 4-fold symmetry.
content_words: rotational symmetry feature map defect detection per head

matches: rotation-equivariant attention (VN-Transformer, Group Equivariant Self-Attention, PSEAD)
rotational symmetry defect detection (PSC-YOLO, Device Defect Intrinsic Rotational Symmetry)
group-averaging projection / symmetric subspace projection (PSEAD Q/K/V)
4-fold/cyclic rotation equivariance on attention feature maps

decision_rationale: Candidate's MEDALLION-ROT-DEFECT combines (a) per-head 4-fold C4 rotation-equivariance scoring via max Frobenius deviation d_h = max_theta ||F_h - R_theta F_h||_F and (b) group-averaging projection F_h^repair = (1/|G|) sum_theta R_theta F_h. Both ingredients are prior art: PSEAD already projects Q/K/V onto symmetric (group-invariant) subspaces via irreducible decomposition, which is mathematically equivalent to Reynolds/group-averaging onto the trivial-isotypic component; VN-Transformer, Equiformer/V3, and Group Equivariant Self-Attention implement rotation-equivariant attention. On the defect-detection side, PSC-YOLO explicitly uses pinwheel rotational symmetry with a transformer for defect detection, and 'Device Defect Intrinsic Rotational Symmetry' and FAX-Net cover symmetry-based defect scoring. The Persian-carpet medallion domain framing does not constitute a novel mechanism: C4 equivariance + Reynolds averaging applied to attention feature maps is standard. Verdict: FAIL.
